title: Convolvulus stenophyllus
intro: Convolvulus stenophyllus, also known as Chinese bindweed or Chinese morning glory, is a perennial vine plant native to China, belonging to the Convolvulaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and inflammation, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Convolvulus stenophyllus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Convolvulus stenophyllus is often used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to aid in the treatment of fever, arthritis, and other inflammatory disorders.
taxonomy: Convolvulus stenophyllus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae, commonly known as the morning glory family. This family is part of the order Solanales, which also includes other plants like potatoes and eggplants. The genus Convolvulus consists of about 250 species of plants, most of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Convolvulus stenophyllus is endemic to Australia and is a low-growing, herbaceous plant with small, white or pinkish flowers. It is often found growing in sandy or stony areas and is considered to be a relatively rare species.

The plant's extracts have been studied for their ability to exhibit antioxidant and free radical scavenging activities.
